title: Visual critique output
description: Structured output contract for the visual-critic subagent. Since Sprint 07, 10 scoring dimensions use a 0-10 numeric scale (replacing the legacy 1-5 integer scale documented in critique-schema.md). The 10th dimension, content_resilience, measures how well the component survives realistic data (long names, empty states, nullable fields) and is null when no visionary-kit.json is present in the project. The 'confidence' object mirrors each dimension with a 1-5 self-reported certainty used by early-exit gating and pairwise tie-breaking.

round.description: Which critique iteration this output corresponds to.

scores.description: Per-dimension score on a 0-10 scale. 10 = exemplary, 0 = critical failure. Use decimals where warranted (e.g. 7.5).

craft_measurable.description: Ninth dimension: numeric_scores.composite × 10. MUST be null when numeric_scores.enabled is false or numeric_scores.composite is null (scorer disabled / sharp unavailable / DOM snapshot empty). Required key so the slot is always present; nullable so early-exit gating can tolerate degraded mode.

content_resilience.description: Tenth dimension (Sprint 07): how well the component survives realistic data. Composite of 0.4×layout_holds + 0.4×empty_state_quality + 0.2×typography_robustness, each measured by rendering against p50 / p95 / empty content from visionary-kit.json. MUST be null when no kit is present (no kit = no realism baseline to score against).

confidence.description: 1-5 integer certainty per dimension. Consumed by early-exit gating (shouldEarlyExit requires min(confidence) >= 4) and pairwise tie-breaking. Keys SHOULD mirror 'scores' but are not required to — unknown keys are ignored.

top_3_fixes.description: Up to 3 highest-impact fixes ordered by impact. Empty array is valid when convergence_signal=true and no fixes are useful.

dimension.description: Which of the 10 scored dimensions this fix targets.

severity.description: blocker = must be fixed before ship; major = should be fixed; minor = nice-to-have.

proposed_fix.description: One concrete actionable change. No 'improve X' generalities.

selector_hint.description: CSS selector or DOM hint when the fix targets a specific element. Duplicates evidence.value when evidence.type == 'selector' — kept for backwards compatibility with Sprint 2 consumers.

evidence.description: Mechanical citation grounding the critique in observable data. Sprint 3 mandate: no evidence, no sub-7 score.

type.description: axe = axe-core rule or node ID; selector = CSS selector present in DOM; metric = numeric_scores key; coord = 'x,y' or computed CSS value.

value.description: The cited identifier. Format depends on `type`: axe rule id ('color-contrast'), CSS selector ('.hero h1'), metric key ('contrast_entropy=0.41'), or coordinate ('x=872,y=142').

convergence_signal.description: True when this round's quality regressed vs the previous round beyond the oscillation threshold (>0.3 delta on overall score). When true, the caller reverts to the previous round's output and stops iterating.

slop_detections.description: Slop patterns detected in this round. pattern_id references the 26-pattern catalogue in critique-schema.md.

pattern_id.description: Row number from the 26-pattern catalogue (1-20 deterministic, 21-26 vision-required).

axe_violations_count.description: Number of axe-core violations detected across all impact levels. Consumed by early-exit gating (requires axe_violations_count === 0).

numeric_scores.description: Output of the deterministic numeric-aesthetic-scorer (Sprint 3 Item 7). All sub-scores are 0..1 or null when the scorer could not compute them (sharp missing, DOM empty, etc.). The critique MUST address any non-null sub-score below 0.7 in top_3_fixes with evidence.type='metric'.

prompt_hash.description: SHA-256 of the visual-critic system prompt + schema bytes that produced this critique (Sprint 3 Task 9.4). Consumed by runtime calibration to detect when calibration.json was fit against a different prompt version and must not be applied. When two critics disagree on their prompt hash (Sprint 06 multi-critic merge), the joined form 'sha256:xxx||sha256:yyy' is permitted.

bon_stats.description: Best-of-N fan-out metrics (Sprint 4 Task 10.4). Present only when the round used BoN. Produced by hooks/scripts/lib/fork-candidate.mjs#collectBonStats. Optional block — omit when the round ran sequential refine.

rounds_using_bon.description: Number of rounds in this generation that fanned out to 3 candidates. Typically 0 (BoN off), 1 (round 2 only), or 2 (rounds 2 and 3).

avg_verifier_margin.description: Dominant margin across all fan-out rounds (tiebreak-conservative: narrow wins over decisive).

escalations_to_user.description: Number of rounds where the verifier set escalate_to_user: true (all three candidates surfaced).

candidate_failures.description: Total count of candidates that failed to produce a complete artefact set across all BoN rounds.

winner_score_lift_vs_seq.description: Delta between winner's calibrated craft_measurable and the pre-BoN sequential baseline composite. Positive = BoN helped; negative = BoN hurt; null = no baseline available.

verifier_prompt_hash.description: SHA-256 of the visual-verifier system prompt bytes. Tracked separately from the critic's prompt_hash — verifier changes do not invalidate calibration.
